text.title: Common Hook Patterns
text.summary: Six production-ready hook patterns with event, matcher, and implementation notes.

rule.statement: PostToolUse + matcher "Edit" (and separately "Write"): read file_path from stdin JSON, detect extension, run formatter, always exit 0. Skip files larger than 1MB. Log errors to /tmp/claude-hooks.log, never to stderr.
rule.rationale: stderr output appears in Claude's context as noise. Error file logging keeps it invisible to the session while still diagnosable. Files larger than 1MB are likely generated or binary — formatters should not process them.

example.note: Formatter hook: detect .py extension → ruff format + ruff check --fix --quiet. Detect .ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx/.json → npx prettier --write. Detect .go → gofmt -w. Each formatter guarded by command -v check to skip gracefully if not installed.

rule.statement: UserPromptSubmit + SubagentStart: check if running in tmux ($TMUX is set), capture pane content to a timestamped file, keep only last 10 captures. This preserves session context across context compaction events.
rule.rationale: Context compaction can discard tool output history. Tmux captures provide a fallback log of what Claude was doing when compaction occurred, enabling session resume without full context loss.

rule.statement: PreToolUse + matcher "Bash": parse the command from stdin JSON, block: (1) git push --force to main/master; (2) git clean -f; (3) rm -rf / or rm -rf ~; (4) shutdown/reboot. Return {"action":"block","reason":"..."} with a specific reason per rule.
rule.rationale: These commands are irreversible and frequently generated by agents operating in unfamiliar environments. The safety guard is the last line of defense before execution, not a substitute for careful prompting.

rule.statement: PostToolUse + matcher "Edit": skip non-.py files, skip test files (test_*.py, *_test.py), find project root via pyproject.toml or setup.py, locate corresponding test file, run pytest with -x -q --tb=short. Always exit 0.
rule.rationale: Auto-test after edit surfaces regressions immediately rather than at commit time. The skip-test-files guard prevents infinite loops when Claude edits a test file, triggering the test, which triggers another edit.

rule.statement: PostToolUse + matcher "Write": check if file is in a git repo, check git status for "??" (untracked), git add only if untracked. Never stage modified files — only newly created ones.
rule.rationale: Auto-staging modified files would stage partial work. The "??" check restricts staging to new files only, which is the case where forgetting to git add is most common.

text.reference: Full auto-format hook script (Python, JS/TS, Go, Rust)
